title: Repeatable Live Mission Cell Proof V1
summary: Rerun the hardened mission-cell path on the same bounded target so DGDH proves repeatability instead of stopping at one good hardening cut.

charter.objective: Run the same long-autonomy continuity path twice with one hardening cut in between, then prove on the second pass that the original friction was actually reduced.
charter.primaryMetric: A mission launched from `missionCell: repeatable-live-mission-cell-proof-v1` completes live run A, hardening, live run B, and promotion without needing the same level of manual mission restating on the second pass.
guardMetrics: No first-green closure is mistaken for mission completion.
No Copilot/editor internal artifact reads happen unless the task is explicit forensics.
No unrelated platform or provider expansion is opened.
charter.budget: One long autonomous branch-sized mission where midpoint commits are allowed but strong success requires a rerun.
charter.runtime: A single proving container that must include a second live pass before promotion completes.
allowedZones: targeted tests and bounded live proof
forbiddenZones: editor or Copilot internals forensics without explicit permission

type2Autonomy: bounded continuity and mission-state truth improvements
targeted runtime, CLI, shared, or server cuts that stay reversible
repeatable rerun proof on the same path
type1Escalations: main branch mutation outside reviewed merge flow
global policy changes beyond this mission cell
escalationPath: stop at the boundary instead of guessing
state the exact action and why it is Type-1
escalate to David or the Oberreviewer gate with concrete evidence

oberreviewerTriggers: the rerun would widen into a new platform or new mission
promotion is being claimed without live run B
the same friction repeats and suggests the current mission cell still hides too much runtime truth
requiredEvidence: targeted tests, replay truth, or runtime proof
the smallest truthful statement of what changed between live run A and live run B
stopReasons: remaining work has widened into a new program

replayChecks: live run B follows the same root path as live run A
the hardened continuity seam appears in prompt or wakeup truth without manual restating
the original friction is reduced on the second pass
guardChecks: promotion does not happen before live run B
Copilot/editor internals are not used as ordinary truth surfaces
the mission remains inside the same bounded target
successSignals: live run B needs less mission restating than live run A
the second pass confirms the hardening really helped
the next mission can reuse the same path more cleanly

defaultTargets: the minimal runtime seam that now carries the rerun truth durably
promoteWhen: live run B shows the original friction is smaller
the same path is easier to carry from repo truth on the second pass
the proof is reviewable without editor-internal archaeology
stayExperimentalWhen: live run B did not happen
the result still depends on manual retelling
the proof depends on forbidden side surfaces instead of normal runtime truth
demoteWhen: the same friction still appears unchanged on the second pass
the mission needs the same restating despite the hardening
promotion would hide that rerun truth is still missing

firmBound: the requirement for live run B
the repeatability definition of strong success
the ban on non-forensic editor-artifact reads
the continuity and promotion truth that survives the second pass
carrierBound: current CLI and portability loading path

starterPath.trigger: Create or update a bounded self-improvement issue with `missionCell: repeatable-live-mission-cell-proof-v1` and a clear rerun condition in `doneWhen`.
issueTemplate: Objective: <same bounded target as live run A>
doneWhen: <live run A + hardening + live run B + promotion condition>
startupSequence: Validate the mission cell contract with `paperclipai mission cell validate`.
Reference the mission cell directly in the issue packet.
State the same bounded target and the exact rerun proof expected after hardening.
Carry live run A, then hardening, then live run B on the same path.
Promote only after the second pass proves the improvement.
firstProbe: Confirm the mission packet explicitly requires a second live pass.
Confirm the same root path is being used rather than a new easier side path.
Use normal runtime truth surfaces, not editor-internal artifacts.
